prompt: Create a topic-specific editorial photograph for a practical article about pairing a Matter smart-home device directly to Home Assistant without installing the device maker's app. Show a warm, lived-in residential entryway with an oak console, soft plaster wall, linen and woven textures, and natural late-afternoon window light. A homeowner's hand holds a modern phone above a small unbranded Matter-style smart plug and a compact white motion sensor on the oak surface, as if scanning a physical setup code card. Turn the card partly away so no code, words, logo, or symbols are readable. Place a compact unbranded local smart-home hub and router nearby, with a warm table lamp switched on. Use realistic materials, believable device proportions, a wide landscape composition that keeps the phone, setup card, smart plug, sensor, local hub, router, and glowing lamp legible in both the full image and a centered article-card crop. Use warm cream, oak, linen, matte white and charcoal hardware, with restrained dark-green accents. No embedded words, readable screens, QR patterns, numbers, headlines, logos, brand marks, watermark, invented branded hardware, decorative routing or connection overlays, diagrams, translucent panels, inset frames, floating UI cards, progress bars, fake charts, poster treatment, floating devices, app-store screens, ecommerce scenes, logo collections, neon, blue-purple gradients, duplicated hardware, surreal hands, pseudo-text, cable clutter, or giant phones.
